front: Philosopher: argued reality exists only in the mind ('to be is to be perceived'); the 'master argument' uses trees in a park and books in a closet to deny mind-independent objects
work: Subjective Idealism and the Master Argument

front: Philosopher: collapsed Locke's distinction between primary and secondary qualities by arguing heat and color are experienced as pleasure and pain
work: Subjective Idealism and the Master Argument

front: Work: dialogue between Philonous ('lover of mind') defending immaterialism and Hylas ('matter') representing Locke's position
back: Three Dialogues Between Hylas and Philonous (George Berkeley)
work: Three Dialogues Between Hylas and Philonous

front: Work: attacks the foundations of infinitesimal calculus, ridiculing derivatives as 'ghosts of departed quantities'; addressed to an 'infidel mathematician'
work: The Analyst and Critique of Calculus

front: Philosopher: this thinker's Essay Towards a New Theory of Vision proposed the 'heterogeneity thesis' that sight and touch detect distinct, incommensurable objects
Philosopher: David Stove called the 'master argument' by this thinker 'The Worst Argument in the World'; Andre Gallois coined the name
work: Subjective Idealism and the Master Argument
